agent_idea: Fuse the first two butterfly layers (layers 0 and 1) in `first_half_general` and `first_half_general_oop` into a single memory pass (`dit_fused_layers_0_1` and `dit_fused_layers_0_1_oop`), processing 4 consecutive rows at once and applying both butterfly layers in registers before writing back, reducing total memory traffic by eliminating one full-matrix read+write pass per coset DFT invocation.

Extend the existing 2-layer (0+1) butterfly fusion in `first_half_general` and `first_half_general_oop` to fuse 3 layers (0+1+2), processing 8 rows at a time in registers and eliminating one additional full-matrix memory read+write pass per coset DFT invocation.

Fuse the last two layers (layer_rev=0 and layer_rev=1) of `second_half_general` into a single memory pass, processing groups of 4 rows at a time with all three twiddles applied in registers before writing back, eliminating one full-matrix read+write pass per `second_half_general` call (2 calls per `coset_lde_batch`).

Fuse layers 0 and 1 in the non-general `first_half` function (used in the inverse DFT portion of `coset_lde_batch`) into a single memory pass, exploiting that all layer-0 twiddles are 1 (no multiply) and layer-1's first twiddle is 1, reducing the fused kernel to just 1 Montgomery multiply per 4 rows while eliminating one full-matrix read+write pass over the 2^20 × 256 inverse DFT data.

agent_idea: Batch the `first_half_general_oop` across all out-of-place coset DFTs in `coset_lde_batch` so that the shared source matrix (1 GB for 2^20×256 BabyBear) is read from DRAM once instead of N-1 times (N=4 for typical 4× blowup), saving ~2 GB of DRAM bandwidth by keeping the source in L3 cache across coset computations within each parallel chunk.

Replace `vpminud` (port 0 only) with `vpcmpud` (port 5) + masked add/sub in the AVX512 packed Add and Sub implementations, relieving port 0 pressure from Montgomery multiplications in the butterfly hot loop.

Move the 1/N scaling from the first butterfly layer (largest blocks, cold data) to the last butterfly layer (smallest blocks, warm cache) in `second_half` of the inverse DFT, keeping the initial memory-bandwidth-heavy passes lean (1 mul per element instead of 2 for ScaledDitButterfly) while the extra multiplication at the last layer is "free" because the data is still hot in L1/L2 cache from the previous layer.

agent_idea: Move the 1/N scaling from the first butterfly layer (largest blocks, cold data) to the last butterfly layer (smallest blocks, data hot in L1) in `second_half` of the inverse DFT, reducing compute pressure on the bandwidth-bottlenecked initial pass while the extra multiplication at the cache-hot last layer is essentially free — with a proper guard (`if mid < log_h`) to prevent usize underflow on `log_h - 1` when `log_h == 0`, which was the cause of the test failure in experiment 7.

agent_idea: Replace `chunks_mut`/`chunks` with `chunks_exact_mut`/`chunks_exact` throughout all butterfly layer functions and cache the first input value in `DitButterfly::apply_to_rows`/`apply_to_rows_oop` to eliminate potential redundant loads, communicating exact-size invariants to the compiler for better bounds-check elimination and loop optimization.

Remove the `backwards` zigzag alternation from all butterfly layer functions, always iterating forward through blocks. This simplifies codegen (eliminates dead branch in every layer), reduces code duplication (each function body is ~40% smaller), and removes the `DoubleEndedIterator` requirement — while being provably correct since `.rev()` on `.zip()` preserves block-twiddle pairings and all block computations are independent.

Batch all OOP coset DFTs' first_half into a single parallel pass that reads each source chunk once from DRAM and processes all cosets from L2/L3 cache, reducing source reads from N to 1 per chunk (saving ~2 GB DRAM bandwidth for 4× blowup) while keeping each coset's data hot in L2 for its subsequent in-place butterfly layers.

Precompute all coset twiddles and pass them directly to DFT functions (eliminating `get_or_compute_coset_twiddles` lock+BTreeMap lookups from the critical path between sequential coset DFTs), and remove the `&Radix2DitParallel` parameter from inner DFT functions to reduce argument passing overhead and potentially improve inlining.
